destinations.evidence: The verifier enforces inclusive TTL bounds, rejects unsupported precompile metadata, and rejects zero executor addresses before accepting an async commitment as structurally valid.
After structural checks, the verifier validates and decodes the precompile payload and rejects executor or TTL drift between the payload and commitment fields.

equivalence_argument: The verifier accepts commitments only after checking TTL in 1..=MAX_SHORT_ASYNC_TTL_BLOCKS, resolving supported async precompile metadata, rejecting zero executor addresses, validating the precompile payload, and confirming decoded executor/TTL match the commitment fields. Each invalid path returns an error instead of panicking.
notes: The v2 supported set excludes unported fee families such as audio/video/FHE from commitment acceptance.
rationale: The commit replaces TODO tests with concrete unit and property tests for TTL lower/upper bounds, valid precompile addresses, zero executor rejection, valid commitment acceptance, and no panics on arbitrary commitment structure inputs. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async commitment validation must accept commitments only when ttl is in the inclusive range [1, MAX_TTL_BLOCKS], the precompile address is one of the supported async precompiles, and executor_address is nonzero; invalid TTL, invalid precompile address, or zero executor address must be rejected without panicking.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run validate_commitment_structure against commitments with ttl 0, ttl MAX_TTL_BLOCKS + 1, an unsupported precompile address, and Address::ZERO executor and assert the expected errors; run valid combinations across all supported precompiles and assert Ok with no panics under fuzzed inputs.
summary: Async commitment structural constraints are enforced inline.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run validate_commitment_structure against commitments with ttl 0, ttl MAX_TTL_BLOCKS + 1, an unsupported precompile address, and Address::ZERO executor and assert the expected errors; run valid combinations across all supported precompiles and assert Ok with no panics under fuzzed inputs.
title: Async commitments obey structural validity constraints
